title: Moderately Crack Theory Wes Anderson is making a claymation Evangelion
display_title: Moderately Crack Theory Wes Anderson is making a claymation Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Moderately Crack Theory: Wes Anderson is making a claymation Evangelion             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Fri Oct 28, 2016 2:41 pm Wes Anderson wants to do sci-fi. And he's interested in Evangelion. Wes Anderson has secretly started making a new animated movie ...and no-one knows what it's about. IS THIS IT? (I mean, I know it's not...) But what if it's Eva-y, you know?

[]: Re: Moderately Crack Theory: Wes Anderson is making a claymation Evangelion             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Oct 29, 2016 7:42 am The first article was written way back in 2009. Wes Anderson has directed two feature-length films since then, neither of them were science-fiction nor were they photographed in space. The second articles says that it's going to be be a movie about a dog. I don't see how that can inherently lead to a sci-fi space romp, so I doubt this is anything significantly Evangelion-related outside of maybe the narrative work addressing the same themes.

[]: Re: Moderately Crack Theory: Wes Anderson is making a claymation Evangelion             Quote Post by Director Black » Sat Oct 29, 2016 9:20 pm Considering that he's actually a fan of Eva, it be weird to see him take it on. I love a lot of his movies, but a indie filmmaker who's sets come out of a lavish dollhouse is the last person I'd expect (Though it would be a great Youtube Parody)

[]: Re: Moderately Crack Theory: Wes Anderson is making a claymation Evangelion             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Sun Oct 30, 2016 5:46 pm So there's Wes Anderson X Men Wes Anderson Shining Aka 'Grand Overlook Hotel' Wes Anderson Space Odyssey 2001 Wes Anderson Star Wars Wes Anderson Full Metal Jacket And yet no End of Andersongelion: You Can (NOT) Display Exquisite Penmanship. Some people have too much time on their hands...and some people not enough.

[]: Re: Moderately Crack Theory: Wes Anderson is making a claymation Evangelion             Quote Post by Director Black » Sun Oct 30, 2016 6:00 pm ^ Don't forget about his take on horror movies! https://vimeo.com/129804479 S.U.B.: If I make it as a director, I can hopefully promise to you that I will attempt to bring Anderson's vision of Evangelion as a short film. It may sound pretentious, but hey, this is an indie filmmaker taking on one of the most philisophical anime that's ever existed.

[]: Re: Moderately Crack Theory: Wes Anderson is making a claymation Evangelion             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Thu Nov 03, 2016 1:58 am Wes Anderson's new film is a stop motion feature about dogs inspired by the works of Yasujiro Ozu... through Wes Anderson's own sensibilities of course. It will be amazing I'm sure.

[]: Re: Moderately Crack Theory: Wes Anderson is making a claymation Evangelion             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Nov 03, 2016 12:03 pm I could get behind an expanded universe sort of deal, say the eccentric science-hipsters of MIT who built unit 3 amidst existential woes and dramatic interpersonal happenstance. Bill Murray has to voice the base commander, obviously.

[]: Re: Moderately Crack Theory: Wes Anderson is making a claymation Evangelion             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Thu Nov 03, 2016 12:15 pm Inorite? It's like the only part of Evangelion Wessie likes is that movie where all the explosions are just metaphors for existential crisis. He probably doesn't even like giant robots!

[]: Re: Moderately Crack Theory: Wes Anderson is making a claymation Evangelion             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Tue Nov 15, 2016 9:56 am shinjeh get in the fucken colonnade funicular

[]: Re: Moderately Crack Theory: Wes Anderson is making a claymation Evangelion             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sun Dec 04, 2016 6:23 am I was hoping Disney would do it so I could hear Shinji sing.

[]: Re: Moderately Crack Theory: Wes Anderson is making a claymation Evangelion             Quote Post by Luigi shinji » Sun Dec 04, 2016 7:05 am Tumbling Down wrote: I was hoping Disney would do it so I could hear Shinji sing.

[]: Re: Moderately Crack Theory: Wes Anderson is making a claymation Evangelion             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Wed Dec 21, 2016 2:38 pm New project described as 'a Japanese movie' ...I think he's toying with me now.
